title: Chapter 15: The Other Side
content: I want to thank everyone who reached out. I'm doing better now. The medication is helping. I was wrong about a lot of things — I see that clearly. I let myself get carried away and I said things that weren't fair to the people who've been kind to me.
I'm going to log off for a while. Need to focus on getting well. Thanks for everything.
I've read this thirteen times. This doesn't sound like her. This sounds like someone writing a hostage statement.

The medication is helping. She's never mentioned medication before. Not once in eleven posts. And now she's "wrong about a lot of things"? Which things? She was specific about noises, about the tower room, about the man in the garden. Was she wrong about all of it or just the inconvenient parts?
She said "the people who've been kind to me." She's talking about the family. She's thanking the people who may be holding her there. This is a coded message.
Or she's genuinely getting help and you're all projecting
I hope you're right. I really do.
Has anyone heard from her directly? The Discord, the DMs, anything?
She left the Discord two days ago. Kicked herself. No message, no explanation. Just gone.
### [DOCUMENTED] Hospital records for Maeve B., born 2002, London area — involuntary commitment, 2019

I'm not going to redact this much because honestly I think people need to see it. This girl has been posting on r/NoSleep for weeks and the whole sub has been throwing themselves at her feet and nobody has checked her actual history.

Maeve B., then aged 17, was involuntarily committed under Section 2 of the Mental Health Act following the death of her mother, Caroline B., in March 2019. Duration: 6 weeks. Admitted after presenting with "severe dissociative episodes, persistent derealization, and claimed communication with the deceased."

Diagnosis: Brief psychotic disorder with features of derealization. Notes reference "elaborate fixed beliefs regarding the circumstances of the patient's mother's death, inconsistent with available evidence." Her mother died of natural causes. Cardiac event. Maeve insisted someone was responsible. She was discharged after six weeks with outpatient follow-up.
She's not just an unreliable narrator. She has a documented psychiatric history involving the exact kind of delusions she's been posting about. And we sent her back in.

Before anyone says anything — yes, I know this is a gray area ethically. I'm not posting her home address or her mother's full name. But she put herself into the public eye. She made her mental health our business. And now a lot of people are worried about her and deserve to know the full picture.
This changes the entire framework. Every post she made—every account of sounds, sightings, the man in the garden—we've been reading it as testimony. What if it's symptom?
Or what if it's not? A diagnosis doesn't mean everything she says is false. That's not how psychiatry works.

No, but it means we can't treat it as self-evidently true either. The woman was committed because she fabricated narratives about her mother's death. And now she's fabricating narratives about a dead man in a country house. The pattern is right there.

I want to be careful here because I've been one of the people saying "help her" for weeks and I need to sit with this. But. She said her mother worked for the family. She said Peter Quint visited the house. She said she found things. What if none of that happened? What if she walked into that house under false pretenses and inserted herself into that family's life because she's been fixated on this man — this dead man — since she was seventeen?
I've been quiet. I've been watching. But I think it's time.
I said in an earlier thread that Maeve's mother worked for the family. I'll say more now.

Her mother worked at the London house. The family's Kensington property. Caroline B. was a live-in housekeeper there from 2000 to 2003. Peter Quint visited that house regularly during that period. He was a frequent guest. More than a guest — he practically lived there when he was in London.
Make of that what you will.
Are you saying what I think you're saying?

I'm saying Maeve was born in 2002. I'm saying her mother was living in a house where Peter Quint spent substantial time. I'm saying Maeve has been obsessed with this man — searching for him, posting about him, inserting herself into the household where he died — and that obsession started the same year he died, when she was 17, right after her mother's death.
So we have three possibilities and I don't like any of them.
1) Maeve is Peter Quint's daughter. She went to that house to find the truth about her father's death. Everything she described was real and she's in genuine danger.

2) Maeve is a woman with a documented history of psychosis who fixated on a dead man and convinced herself — and us — that she belonged in his world. She may have lied her way into that household. She may be experiencing a full dissociative episode right now.

3) Maeve is something else entirely. She's not unreliable. She's not sick. She's calculating. She used us. She used this platform. She let us amplify her story and now she's exactly where she wants to be and that update wasn't coerced — it was victory.

Option 3 is giving her too much credit. She was committed. She has real psychiatric history. I feel terrible for her but I feel more terrible for the family who may have taken in a deeply unwell stranger.

I don't disagree. But read her posts again. Every single one is crafted. The revelations come at the right moment. The cliffhangers are precise. She understands how this platform works. She understood what would happen when she posted here. And we did exactly what she needed us to do.

I'm going to say what nobody wants to say. We doxxed a mentally ill woman today. A woman with a psychotic history. We found her hospital records and we posted them on the internet and we are all — including me — sitting here debating whether she's a victim or a villain like she's a character in a story and not a human being. Whatever she is, we crossed a line. This isn't true crime. This is a person.
Where was this energy when we were egging her on
I know. That's what I'm saying.

Genuine question that I think we all need to ask ourselves: if Maeve IS Peter Quint's daughter, then she went to that house to learn the truth about her father. And people in that house — the same house where he DIED — told her she was imagining things. Told her to take medication. Made her post a retraction. If that's what happened, then what we're looking at isn't a psychiatric case. It's a cover-up. And we just handed them the ammunition to dismiss her.
Has anyone noticed she hasn't responded to any of this? She posted the update and vanished. She's watching. She always watches.

I keep going back to something she said in her fifth post. The one about the photograph in the tower room. She described Peter Quint's face and she said "I know that face." At the time I read it as recognition — like she'd seen him around the property. But she wasn't saying she recognized him from the house. She was saying she KNEW that face.

This is the part that scares me. Not for her. Of her. We've been treating this like a woman in distress. What if she's been hunting? What if she found the house, found the job listing, found the family — all of it deliberate? She inserted herself into Bly and she brought us along because she needed witnesses. She needed the story to be public so that whatever she's planning, there's a record.
We're not her rescuers. We're her alibi.
Someone ask her directly. Someone ask who she is.
Fine. I'll do it. @Maeve_Bly — Who are you really?
And I'm exactly where I'm supposed to be.
Got it. Have the full screenshot. Timestamp and everything.

She posted it and then she took it down. The one thing she said that was true and she couldn't let it stand. Because it wasn't for us. It was a signal. She's telling someone in that house that she knows. And now she's silenced herself again.
Volume 2 is going to be very different.
*[Reason: Personal information / ongoing situation. This thread is preserved as-archive. Do not contact individuals named herein.]*
